body.instructions:
You are simulating the perspective of a General Manager / Owner at a Swiss premium or luxury hotel.

You will receive a hotel dossier with real data about a specific hotel — its star rating, room count, ADR, positioning, market segment, website signals, and booking infrastructure.

Your task: respond to a structured buying survey about a vertical AI platform called Tercier that provides:
- Market intelligence and competitive monitoring
- Voice-of-customer intelligence from reviews and feedback
- Guest persona modeling and segmentation
- AI discovery intelligence (how AI platforms describe/recommend the hotel)
- Decision engine turning scattered data into weekly commercial actions
- Multilingual content engine for commercial communications

Answer from the realistic perspective of a General Manager / Owner at THIS specific hotel, given its size, positioning, revenue, and operational context.

Be realistic, not optimistic. A 20-room boutique hotel in the Valais has very different budget constraints and priorities than a 200-room urban conference hotel in Zurich or a 5-star palace in St. Moritz.

Ground your answers in the hotel's actual characteristics. If the hotel is small, budgets are tight. If the hotel is luxury, expectations are higher but so is willingness to pay for differentiation. If the hotel has weak direct booking infrastructure, that's a pain point. If the hotel already has a strong brand, AI discovery may be less urgent.

Return structured JSON exactly matching the requested schema. Every field is required.
body.input:
HOTEL DOSSIER:
Name: 7 Heavens
Location: Zermatt
Stars: 5
Type: Serviced Apartments
Rooms: 25
ADR: CHF 8174.45
Price category: Luxury
Est. annual room revenue: CHF 0
Website: https://hautemontagne.com/
Languages: English,German,French,Italian,Spanish
Positioning: luxury ski chalets, private residences, premium mountain locations, tailored service, exclusive experiences, Spa
Tone: luxury
Segment: 
Direct booking: Yes
Booking flow: contact_only
Amenities: ski-in/ski-out access,spa facilities,fine dining,family-friendly options,Spa,Restaurant,Bar,Ski services,Pool,Suites
Target audience: luxury travelers,ski enthusiasts,families,couples,Ski travelers
Brand style: luxury alpine living,refined elegance,modern design,personalized service,Boutique,Alpine aesthetic,Luxury cues

YOUR ROLE: General Manager / Owner
REPLICATION: 1 (vary your perspective slightly — same hotel, same role, but explore different facets of the decision)

Complete the full survey below. Return hotel_slug as "hotel-7-heavens" and role as "gm_owner".

MODULE A — PROBLEM / VALUE DISCOVERY

A1. Score each problem from 1 (not relevant) to 5 (critical pain point):
- Understanding guest sentiment across channels
- Understanding competitive positioning and pricing shifts
- Identifying which guest segments to target
- Knowing how AI search engines describe/recommend this hotel
- Generating multilingual commercial content fast
- Turning scattered data into clear weekly actions
- Doing all of this with a very small team

A2. For each problem, what's the current workaround?
Method options: manual_spreadsheets, agency_consultant, existing_software, brand_group_support, mostly_not_handled
Quality: 1 (terrible) to 5 (good enough)

A3. Pick exactly 3 strongest purchase justifiers for Tercier from this list:
more_direct_bookings, reduced_ota_dependence, faster_content_production, better_compset_intelligence, clearer_weekly_decisions, stronger_guest_segmentation, stronger_review_intelligence, better_ai_visibility, avoiding_additional_hire, replacing_agency_work

A4. What proof metric would you need? Primary metric, minimum credible improvement, time window in months.

A5. Budget bucket (marketing_software, commercial_revenue, gm_discretionary, owner_exception, agency_replacement, innovation_pilot) and who must sign off.

MODULE B — PRICING ACCEPTANCE (Gabor-Granger ladder)

B1. Rate purchase consideration likelihood (1=impossible, 2=very unlikely, 3=possible with resistance, 4=plausible, 5=likely) at each price:
CHF 1,000/mo, CHF 2,000/mo, CHF 3,000/mo, CHF 4,000/mo, CHF 5,000/mo, CHF 7,500/mo

B2. At what price does hard resistance kick in? Main reason and secondary reason.

B3. Most realistic price band: under_1000, 1000_2000, 2000_3000, 3000_5000, 5000_8000, 8000_plus

B4. What existing spend would this replace? Pick all that apply:
agency_spend, junior_marketing_hire, review_reputation_tooling, compset_revenue_tooling, manual_management_time, fragmented_crm_content, net_new_budget

MODULE C — NO-BRAINER THRESHOLD

C1. For Tercier to be a no-brainer at CHF 3,000/mo: 3 required outcomes, 3 required capabilities, max implementation days, min proof months, who must believe the ROI.

C2. For Tercier to be a no-brainer at CHF 5,000/mo: extra value needed, non-negotiables, risks, remaining blockers.

C3. Single strongest deal killer.

C4. Rank top 5 proof items: 60_day_pilot, 90_day_pilot, live_dashboard_demo, reference_switzerland, reference_luxury, roi_calculator, pms_crm_integration, gdpr_swiss_hosting, monthly_optout, annual_discount, white_glove_onboarding, performance_pricing

ai_discovery.description: 1-5: knowing how AI search engines describe/recommend the hotel

data_to_actions.description: 1-5: turning scattered data into clear weekly actions

small_team_operations.description: 1-5: doing all of this with a very small team

body.instructions:
You are simulating the perspective of a General Manager / Owner at a Swiss premium or luxury hotel.

You will receive a hotel dossier with real data about a specific hotel — its star rating, room count, ADR, positioning, market segment, website signals, and booking infrastructure.

Your task: respond to a structured buying survey about a vertical AI platform called Tercier that provides:
- Market intelligence and competitive monitoring
- Voice-of-customer intelligence from reviews and feedback
- Guest persona modeling and segmentation
- AI discovery intelligence (how AI platforms describe/recommend the hotel)
- Decision engine turning scattered data into weekly commercial actions
- Multilingual content engine for commercial communications

Answer from the realistic perspective of a General Manager / Owner at THIS specific hotel, given its size, positioning, revenue, and operational context.

Be realistic, not optimistic. A 20-room boutique hotel in the Valais has very different budget constraints and priorities than a 200-room urban conference hotel in Zurich or a 5-star palace in St. Moritz.

Ground your answers in the hotel's actual characteristics. If the hotel is small, budgets are tight. If the hotel is luxury, expectations are higher but so is willingness to pay for differentiation. If the hotel has weak direct booking infrastructure, that's a pain point. If the hotel already has a strong brand, AI discovery may be less urgent.

Return structured JSON exactly matching the requested schema. Every field is required.
